sourceMovie.title: Futureworld
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Futureworld (1976) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Matrix Reloaded
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Futureworld clicked for you, The Matrix Reloaded (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Futureworld clicked for you, The Matrix Revolutions (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Futureworld?
faq.answer: Try The Matrix Reloaded, The Matrix Revolutions, Gemini Man—each shares DNA with Futureworld while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Futureworld?
faq.answer: Futureworld (1976) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Futureworld part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Westworld Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
